rag_rules: Place implementation-level AGREE annex blocks in the target component implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use assign statements only for implementation-level bindings when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Preserve the existing AADL architecture by not modifying component types, implementations, features, connections, or properties
generic_rules: Maintain consistent scope and visibility rules when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Preserve the original AADL model structure during fusion
Do not introduce new component types, implementations, features, or connections during fusion
Ensure that all inserted AGREE annex blocks are valid in their target scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not modify the existing AADL architecture during fusion
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
